text: The Inventory Management System shall provide a categorized view of the entire inventory to the Construction Junction staff.
The system shall display inventory departments and categories in a matrix format.
The system shall list departments and categories in alphabetical order in each row from left to right and top to bottom.
The system shall allow Administrators to add, edit, and delete inventory departments.
The Department Name shall be unique across the inventory.
The POS Department Code shall be at most 3 characters long.
The Department Unique Tag shall be at most 18 characters long.
The system shall prevent the deletion of a Department if it contains categories or items.
The system shall allow Administrators to add, edit, delete, and move inventory categories.
The Category Name shall be unique across the inventory.
The system shall add a corresponding item to QuickBooks POS when a Stock Item Category is created.
The system shall combine Item Details when the option to merge contents of selected categories is selected during a move.
The system shall allow Administrators to define and edit item attributes for each department.
The system shall allow Administrators to define and edit item details for each department or category.
The Item Attribute Name shall be unique across the inventory.
The system shall support Number, Text, and Selection as detail types.
The system shall allow users to add unique and stock items to the inventory.
The system shall update QuickBooks POS promptly when an item is added to the inventory.
The system shall create a new item entry in POS for each unique item added.
The system shall generate item tags containing CJ logo, department name, category name, description, condition, size, price, item number, bar code, acquisition number, and date received.
The system shall allow authorized users to modify, adjust quantity, split, and view history for inventory items.
The system shall require a reason when an item quantity is decreased.
The system shall maintain a history of all price changes and include an explanation for the change.
The system shall record item added, price set, price changed, item sold, quantity adjusted, and item split events in the item history.
The system shall calculate suggested prices based on historical original and sale prices of similar items.
The system shall allow users to view past and current acquisitions from the CRM system.
The system shall allow users to filter acquisitions by type, donor name, acquisition number, phone number, and status.
The system shall synchronize changes to acquisition information between the CRM and the Inventory Management System immediately.
The system shall allow Receiving Associates to process donated items and generate donation receipts.
The system shall record the total amount of time taken to process each acquisition.
The system shall decrement the inventory quantity on hand when a sale is processed in QuickBooks POS.
The system shall generate reports for inventory items, departments, categories, acquisitions, and donors.
The system shall provide a web browser-based interface for all inventory management functionality.
The application shall be optimized for touch screen user interfaces.
The system shall be available during normal Construction Junction operating hours for internal users.
The system shall require a user login for access.
The system shall perform with predictably low response times to avoid impacting staff tasks.
The system shall support bar code readers, Zebra printers, and touch screen monitors.
The system shall synchronize constituent and sales data with Salesforce CRM.
The system shall display a copyright notice in all user interfaces.
